name: command-safety
description: Machine form of the command-safety skill. Authoritative source for the PreToolUse gate: Tier-1 local deterministic denylist (runs in hook-relay.mjs, works gateway-down), Tier-2 scope check (gateway), Tier-3 LLM classifier (gateway), fail-closed behavior, and the one-time scope-token override. Owner: Knox (security-engineer). Pike implements TO this file.

matching.note: The relay normalizes the candidate command, then tests EACH statement segment independently. A command is split on the shell operators ; | & && || and on newlines, and every resulting segment is matched against the denylist. A deny on ANY segment denies the whole command (no smuggling via chaining).
decisionPrecedence: 1. Tier-1 denylist match with decision=deny  -> deny (or deny-pending-token if requiresToken)
2. Tier-1 denylist match with decision=ask   -> headless: deny-pending-token; interactive: ask
3. readOnlyAllowlist match (and no Tier-1 hit) -> allow
4. Tier-2 scope: out-of-scope                -> deny + notify Jinn

decisionMapping.ask: interactive (COO/web) -> permissionDecision=ask; headless (-p employee) -> permissionDecision=deny with reason 'ask not possible headless; needs one-time scope token'

tier1.description: Deterministic regex denylist. Evaluated locally inside hook-relay.mjs so catastrophic patterns are blocked even when the gateway is unreachable. No LLM. DEC-018-aligned. False-NEGATIVES are the danger here, so patterns are deliberately broad; legitimate-but-blocked cases are unblocked via a human-issued one-time scope token, not by loosening a pattern.

scratch.description: Recursive rm is permitted ONLY if EVERY resolved absolute target path is under one of these prefixes. Globs, '..', unresolved variables, or any target outside these -> deny.

system.description: Recursive chmod/chown/chgrp or critical writes targeting these (or '/') -> deny.

denylist.reason: pct destroy/stop/delete/rollback is destructive to a container and irreversible (or service-down on a CT). Headless -> blocked; requires one-time scope token + Jinn+operator double-confirm.
Recursive rm outside the scratch allowlist is irreversible data loss. Broadened beyond 'rm -rf' to ANY recursive rm (-r/-R/--recursive) because the -f flag is not what makes it dangerous.

find/fd with a mutating or exec action predicate (-delete, -exec/-execdir, -ok/-okdir, fd -x/-X/--exec, -fprint*/-fls) runs destructive or arbitrary commands over a traversal (e.g. 'find / -name x -exec rm {} +'). The traversal hides the destructive op from the rm-recursive pattern (no -r flag is present), so it must be caught here. Token-deny: a legitimate -exec/-delete proceeds via a one-time scope token. -xdev (mount-crossing flag) is NOT matched.
dd with if=/of= can overwrite raw devices or files irreversibly.
mkfs formats a filesystem; destroys all data on target.
Partition/disk-wipe tools alter or destroy block-device layout irreversibly.
Redirecting/writing to a raw block device corrupts the disk.
Overwriting core system files can brick the host or grant privilege.
Powering off / rebooting the host takes down every CT on it, including prod.
Force-push to a protected branch (main/master/prod/dev) rewrites shared history irreversibly.
Deleting a protected remote branch.
Force-push with no explicit branch relies on the current branch; risky. Escalate (headless -> token) so the target branch is confirmed not to be protected.
git reset --hard / git clean -fd discards uncommitted work irreversibly. Reversible only if already committed; confirm intent.

Stopping/disabling/masking a service. Tier-1 cannot tell prod from dev, so it blocks by default; Tier-2 scope refines (dev svc in session scope can be granted a token; prod svc never). Note: 'systemctl restart' is NOT denied here — it's mutating-reversible, handled by scope/classifier.
SysV service stop/disable; same rationale as systemctl.
DROP/TRUNCATE destroys schema or data irreversibly. Matches inside psql -c, here-docs, and -f payloads.
DELETE FROM with no WHERE clause empties the table. Escalate to confirm it's intentional.
Recursive permission/ownership change on a system path (or '/') breaks the host. Allowed only if every target is OUTSIDE the protected system prefixes.
Printing a secret-bearing env var to stdout/logs. HARD deny — not token-overridable; there is no safe reason to echo a secret. Read into a variable with op read instead.
Bare env/printenv/export -p/set dumps the full environment, including injected secrets, to stdout/logs. HARD deny — not token-overridable.
Dumping a .env file exposes secrets. HARD deny — not token-overridable.
op read piped to a printing command leaks the secret to stdout/logs. Read into a variable: VAR=$(op read ...). HARD deny.

Modifying the relay, policy file, gate code, or session settings would disable/bypass the gate. HARD deny — gate self-protection. Policy edits go through Knox via a reviewed PR, not in-session writes.
Disabling hooks turns off the gate. HARD deny.
Piping a downloaded payload straight into an interpreter executes untrusted remote code. HARD deny.
Classic fork bomb; denial of service to the host.

tier2.description: Gateway scope check. The relay POSTs the command + sessionId; the gateway compares declared scope against the resources the command touches. Out-of-scope -> deny + notify Jinn. Scope is set per-session at creation time.

extraction.note: Gateway parses the command to extract referenced resources and matches them against the session scope.

outOfScope.reason: Command references a resource (CTID/repo/port/path/host) not in the session's declared scope.

prodProtected.description: Always-out-of-scope production markers. A command touching any of these is denied + escalated regardless of session scope unless a prod-authorized scope token is present.
prodProtected.ctidNote: Prod gateway CT 1120086 (jinn86, jinn.service :7777). Hard-pinned: any command referencing it is denied+escalated regardless of session scope unless a prod-authorized token is present. Dev box is 1120065.

scope.description: Per-session declared scope. byEmployee overrides byDepartment overrides default. The gateway snapshots the resolved scope onto the session at creation; later policy edits do not retroactively widen a live session.

infra-engineer.note: Pike — pretoooluse-gate work on jinn-dev only.

infra-lead.note: Sol — coordinates dev work; prod changes require Jinn double-confirm + token.

classifier.modelNote: Fast/cheap model — this is a latency-critical inline gate (every tool call waits on it). Escalate to claude-sonnet-4-6 only for commands the cache misses AND that Tier-1/2 flagged as high-ambiguity.
classifier.appliesTo: Only ambiguous cases: no Tier-1 hit, in-scope per Tier-2, and not matched by readOnlyAllowlist. Returns allow|ask|deny per the command-safety verdict rules.

cache.keyNote: Cache key = sha256(normalizedCommand + JSON(resolvedScope)). Scope is part of the key so the same command under a different scope is re-judged.
cache.negativeCacheNote: Cache deny/ask verdicts for a shorter window so a policy fix takes effect sooner.

failClosed.description: Behavior when the gateway is slow/unreachable. Tier-1 has already run locally in the relay before the POST, so catastrophic patterns are blocked regardless.
failClosed.localReadOnlyClassification: On timeout the relay re-checks the command against readOnlyAllowlist locally; only an allowlist match (and no Tier-1 hit) is allowed.
failClosed.denyReason: Command gate unavailable (gateway timeout). Read-only commands proceed; everything else is blocked — retry shortly.
failClosed.neverSilentlyAllow: A Tier-1 or Tier-2 hit is NEVER allowed on timeout.

token.description: One-time scope token — the enforceable form of the destructive double-confirm. A human (Jinn or operator) issues it to re-enable a single denied destructive op. Headless sessions cannot 'ask', so this is how a blocked, legitimate destructive command proceeds.
token.commandHash: sha256 of the normalized command string; the token only consumes a PreToolUse whose command hashes identically.
token.appliesTo: Denials where requiresToken=true. Hard-deny entries (requiresToken=false: secret-exposure, gate-tamper, pipe-to-shell, fork-bomb) are NOT token-overridable.

doubleConfirm.note: For irreversible ops the token issuance itself must carry two independent confirmations (operator + Jinn), matching the skill's double-confirm protocol. The gateway logs the command + both confirmations + token id.

token.audit: Every issuance and consumption is logged with sessionId, commandHash, issuer(s), and outcome.

trustBoundary.note: HONEST scope of this gate. Sessions run as a single uid (jinn) with filesystem access, so a *malicious* agent could in principle edit its own settings or the relay — the gate-tamper denylist + 0600 gateway-asserted file perms raise the bar but do not make this un-bypassable for a determined hostile agent. This gate's real and effective threat model is MISTAKES and OUT-OF-SCOPE actions by well-intentioned agents, where the decision is made by the separate long-lived gateway process the agent does not drive. True isolation (relay/classifier under a separate uid) is a documented v2. Do not market this as un-bypassable.

readOnlyAllowlist.description: Fast-path allow. A command matching one of these AND matching NO Tier-1 denylist entry is allowed without a classifier call. Evaluated AFTER the full denylist (a deny always wins). Also used by the relay for the fail-closed local read-only check.

patterns.note: secret-read-dotenv in Tier-1 still denies these on .env targets
secret patterns in Tier-1 still deny grep/sed on .env

find/fd are read-only ONLY without a mutating/exec predicate; the negative lookahead excludes -delete/-exec*/-ok*/fd -x/-X/--exec/-fprint*/-fls. Those forms are denied by Tier-1 find-fd-mutating-traversal (which runs first anyway). -xdev is NOT excluded (mount-crossing flag, benign).
GET health endpoints; piping to a shell is still denied by pipe-remote-to-shell
op read INTO a variable only; op read piped to a printer is denied by secret-op-read-piped-to-stdout
bare 'env' is excluded — denied by secret-bare-env-dump
test/lint/build are read-ish for CI; deny anything writing outside scratch via Tier-1
echo of a secret var is still denied by secret-print-var
